methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $75M total raised proxy.

private.name: Inertia Enterprises
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: DG Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Magrathea Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Stardust Solutions is a Series A-stage energy and climate technology company founded by Yanai Yedvab. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of three private-market peers in the energy and climate sector (Inertia Enterprises, DG Matrix, and Magrathea Metals), with no applicable public comparables at this stage of maturity.

The stage-progression method anchors to Stardust's $75M in total capital raised to date, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression. This approach yields an implied valuation of $150M. Cross-referencing against available market data produced a low-end estimate of $128M, a median of $150M, and a high-end estimate of $172M.

The triangulated range supports a median valuation of $150M, with a credible band between $128M and $172M. This range reflects standard stage-progression mechanics but is constrained by limited comparable data—only one valuation method is currently applicable given the private-market comp set. Key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the three private peers relative to Stardust's specific technology positioning, unit economics, and path to revenue. Further refinement would benefit from revenue-based multiples or DCF anchoring should detailed financial data become available.
